senses.definition: the sound of a bell, or the act of ringing a bell, especially to announce a church service or other event
examples: The bellcast of the church bells signaled the end of the hour.
During the ceremony, the bellcast echoed across the valley.

senses.definition: a small structure at the top of a church wall or roof that contains a bell
examples: The bellcote perched atop the nave, its bell rusted with age.
From the hill, you could see the bellcote against the sky.
synonyms: belfry (smaller term for a bell housing)

senses.definition: plural of bellcote; multiple small bell housings on church roofs
examples: The village church had several bellcotes along the west wall.
Bellcotes are often ornamental as well as functional.

senses.definition: a beautiful woman, often used poetically or in older usage
examples: She was the belle of the ball, radiant in her gown.
The town admired the belle for her grace and charm.

senses.definition: a pretty, fashionable young woman; also used as a nickname or term of endearment
examples: The belle attended the gala with poise.
In the story, the belle wins everyone's attention.

senses.definition: to cause a bell to ring; to ring or announce by bells
examples: The clock belled every hour.
The church bells belled to welcome the parade.

senses.definition: a humorous or playful reference to a domain or place associated with bells; not a standard everyday word, may appear in niche or invented usage
examples: The poem toyed with belledom as a whimsical concept.
In their fantasy world, belledom was a land of ringing towers.

senses.definition: a type of finely crafted Irish pottery (also known as Belleek)
examples: She bought a delicate Belleek teacup as a souvenir.
Belleek porcelain is prized by collectors.

senses.definition: plural of Belleek pottery items
examples: The shelf held several belleeks from the Irish workshop.
Collectors sought belleeks in good condition.

senses.definition: the state or quality of being a belle; social admiration for a beautiful woman
examples: Her charm added to her bellehood at the event.
In old stories, bellehood often brought both attention and gossip.

senses.definition: a plant name (Terminalia bellirica) used in traditional medicine; usage is specialized
examples: Some traditional remedies use belleric to support health.
Herbalists might refer to belleric in compound formulations.

senses.definition: a legendary Greek hero who tamed the winged horse Pegasus; also a scientific genus name in some contexts
examples: Bellerophon defeated the Chimera in myth.
In taxonomy, Bellerophon is sometimes used as a genus name in literature.

senses.definition: a family name in taxonomy (fam. Bellerophontidae), used in paleontology for certain fossil molluscs
examples: Fossils from Bellerophontidae show ancient shell shapes.
The Bellerophontidae family is studied by paleontologists.

senses.definition: plural of belle; more than one woman who is beautiful or admired
examples: The belles gathered for the charity ball.
Two town belles shared the spotlight that night.

senses.definition: a person who makes or sells wood or metal belts; or any craftsman involved with belts
examples: The belletor showed a fine belt he had crafted.
A skilled bellete r can work leather and metal.

senses.definition: plural of bellete r; belts makers or belt sellers
examples: The village had several bellete rs in the market.
Bellete rs displayed their handcrafted belts.

senses.definition: a taste for fine writing, especially literary prose; a literary movement or style
examples: Her interest in belletrism led her to study classic prose.
The salon admired belletrism in contemporary essays.

senses.definition: plural of belletrism; examples of refined literary writing
examples: Her quotations are filled with belletrisms from classic authors.
The anthology features many belletrisms of the era.

senses.definition: a writer of belletristic prose; a literary stylist
examples: He is known as a master belletrist of his generation.
The belletrist published essays on style and form.

senses.definition: relating to belles letters; focused on aesthetic, refined literary prose
examples: The magazine features belletristic essays and poems.
Her belletristic style emphasizes elegance over argument.

senses.definition: relating to belletristic prose; characteristic of refined literary style
examples: Her belletristical remarks delighted the audience.
The book has belletristical flourishes that some readers praise and others find pretentious.

senses.definition: plural of belletrist; writers of belles lettres; literary stylists
examples: The salon gathered several renowned belletrists.
Belletrists often discuss the beauty of prose and poetry.

senses.definition: a Belletrist; a writer of belles lettres; a literary stylist
examples: The bellettrist spoke about the craft of refined prose.
As a bellettrist, she favored elegant phrasing.

senses.definition: plural of bellettrist; writers of belletristic prose
examples: The conference invited several bellettrists to speak on prose technique.
Bellettrists contribute to journals on literary style.

senses.definition: a place name meaning beautiful view; often used as a surname or a geographical name
examples: They live in BelleVue, a town famous for its scenic hills.
The hotel BelleVue offers panoramic city views.

senses.definition: a flowering plant of the campanula family; bell-shaped flowers
examples: The garden bustled with purple bellflowers.
Bellflowers are a popular cottage-garden plant.
